subject: Physics
solution:
From the source $v = V_0 \sin(300t)$, the angular frequency is $\omega = 300\text{ rad/s}$.
Capacitance $C = 100\ \mu\text{F} = 10^{-4}\text{ F}$, so $X_C = \dfrac{1}{\omega C}$.

Case 1 ($S_1$ closed, $S_2$ open):
Closing $S_1$ short-circuits $L_2$, leaving an LCR series circuit with $C$, $R$ and $L_1$. With phase difference $\phi_1 = 30^\circ$:
$\tan 30^\circ = \dfrac{\omega L_1 - X_C}{R}$
$\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \dfrac{\omega L_1 - X_C}{R} \Rightarrow R = \sqrt{3}\,(\omega L_1 - X_C)$ ... (1)

Case 2 ($S_2$ closed, $S_1$ open):
Closing $S_2$ short-circuits $L_1$, leaving an LCR series circuit with $C$, $R$ and $L_2$. With phase difference $\phi_2 = 60^\circ$:
$\tan 60^\circ = \dfrac{\omega L_2 - X_C}{R}$
$\sqrt{3} = \dfrac{\omega L_2 - X_C}{R} \Rightarrow \omega L_2 - X_C = R\sqrt{3}$ ... (2)

Substituting $R$ from (1) into (2):
$\omega L_2 - X_C = \sqrt{3}\,(\omega L_1 - X_C) \cdot \sqrt{3}$
$\omega L_2 - X_C = 3\omega L_1 - 3X_C$
$2X_C = 3\omega L_1 - \omega L_2 = \omega(3L_1 - L_2)$

Therefore:
$3L_1 - L_2 = \dfrac{2X_C}{\omega} = \dfrac{2}{\omega^2 C}$

Substituting $\omega = 300\text{ rad/s}$ and $C = 10^{-4}\text{ F}$:
$\omega^2 C = (300)^2 \cdot 10^{-4} = 9 \times 10^4 \cdot 10^{-4} = 9$

$3L_1 - L_2 = \dfrac{2}{9}\text{ H}$

Hence, the correct option is $(2)\ \dfrac{2}{9}$.

subject: Physics
solution:
Power factor ${ }_{\text {(old) }}$
$=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}{ }^2}}=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+(2 \mathrm{R})^2}}=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{5} \mathrm{R}}$
Power factor ${ }_{\text {(new) }}$
$=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+\left(X_L-X_C\right)^2}}=\frac{R}{\sqrt{R^2+(2 R-R)^2}}$
$=\frac{R}{\sqrt{2} R}$
$\therefore \frac{\text { New power factor }}{\text { Old power factor }}=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{2} \mathrm{R}}}{\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{5} \mathrm{R}}}=\sqrt{\frac{5}{2}}$

subject: Physics

Here, inductive reactance XL=ωL=100π100×10-3=10π Ω

And capacitive reactanceXC=1ωC=1100π×100×10-6=100π Ω=10π Ω

Now, impedance of the series circuit is  Z=R2+XL-XC2=R=10 Ω

Therefore,

IRMS=VRMSZ,

=22010=22 A

solution:
The capacitive reactance of the circuit is given by, 

Xc=1ωC=π2π×50×10-3=10 Ω

The inductive reactance of the circuit is given by, 

XL=ωL=2π×50×100π×10-3=10 Ω

From above calculations, it is clear that

XC=XL

∴ Power factor =RZ=RR=1

subject: Physics
solution:
The impedance of an AC circuit is given by, Z=R2+XC-XL2.

For current to be maximum Z should be minimum. The value of Z will be minimum when XC-XL=0. Therefore, the value of Z=R. Hence, statement C is correct and statement D is wrong.

To the left of ωr i.e. when ω<ωr, XC>XL. Therefore, the circuit is mainly capacitive. Hence, statement A is correct and statement B is wrong.

qc=+Ldidt+iR, as charge decreasing, 


qc=Ld2qdt2-dqdtR

⇒A=q; R=b, m=L
⇒q=q0e-Rt2L⇒q2=q02e-RtL⇒q2=q02e-RtL.

subject: Physics
solution:
For DC voltage
$\mathrm{R}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{I}}=\frac{100}{5}=20 \Omega$
for AC voltage
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}=20 \sqrt{3} \Omega \\
& \mathrm{R}=20 \Omega \\
& \mathrm{Z}=\sqrt{\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2+\mathrm{R}^2}=\sqrt{3 \times 400+400}=40 \Omega \\
& \text { Power }=\mathrm{i}_{\text {rms }}^2 \mathrm{R} \\
& \quad=\left(\frac{\mathrm{V}_{\text {rms }}}{\mathrm{Z}}\right)^2 \times \mathrm{R}=\left(\frac{\frac{200}{\sqrt{2}}}{40}\right)^2 \times 20=250 \mathrm{~W}
\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
In case of series RLC circuit, Equation of voltage is given by
$$
\mathrm{V}^2=V_R^2+\left(V_L-V_C^2\right)
$$
Here, $V=220 V ; V_L=V_C=300 \mathrm{~V}$
$$
\therefore V_R=\sqrt{V^2}=220 \mathrm{~V}
$$
Current $i=\frac{V}{R}=\frac{220}{100}=2.2 \mathrm{~A}$

subject: Physics
solution:
When sinusoidal voltage is applied across LR circuit, current is also sinusoidal but it lags by some phase.

Current will be in the form of
I=I0sinωt-ϕ
Graph will be sinusoidal.

Here I0 is the maximum current and ϕ is phase difference between V & I
